topics.heading: Determine The Theme of A Short Story

questions.story:
Adapted from The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn by Mark Twain

Huck Finn sat alone with the letter in his hand, staring at the words he had written. The candle flickered beside him as the river moved quietly outside. The letter was addressed to Miss Watson, telling her where Jim was hiding.

Jim had run away from slavery, and Huck had helped him escape. Everyone Huck knew had taught him that helping a runaway slave was wrong. If Miss Watson received the letter, Jim would be captured and returned. Huck believed that sending it would make him a respectable boy again.

He imagined how proud people would be if they knew he had done the proper thing. The widow would approve. The town would say Huck Finn had finally behaved like a decent boy.

But when Huck thought of Jim, other memories pushed their way into his mind.

He remembered the nights on the raft when Jim talked about buying his wife and children out of slavery someday. Huck remembered how Jim had stayed awake to watch over him during storms. He remembered Jim calling him his only true friend.

Huck felt miserable.

“All right,” he said slowly to himself, holding the letter. “I’ll do the right thing.”

For a moment, he felt relieved. Then he imagined Jim being dragged back to slavery, and the relief vanished.

Huck picked up the letter again. His hands trembled.

“Well,” he said, taking a deep breath, “if helping Jim means I’m going to hell, then so be it.”

With that, Huck tore the letter into pieces.

He decided he would help Jim escape, no matter what anyone said or believed.
questions.question: Which situation creates the central conflict in this passage?

options.text: Huck cannot decide where he and Jim should travel next along the river.
Huck struggles between obeying society’s rules and protecting his friend Jim.
Huck is afraid that Miss Watson will punish him if he returns home.
Huck is unsure whether Jim truly wants to reunite with his family.

questions.question: What type of conflict is most clearly shown when Huck debates whether to send the letter?

options.text: Person vs. Nature
Person vs. Society
Person vs. Self
Person vs. Person

questions.story:
Adapted from The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn by Mark Twain

Huck Finn sat alone with the letter in his hand, staring at the words he had written. The candle flickered beside him as the river moved quietly outside. The letter was addressed to Miss Watson, telling her where Jim was hiding.

Jim had run away from slavery, and Huck had helped him escape. Everyone Huck knew had taught him that helping a runaway slave was wrong. If Miss Watson received the letter, Jim would be captured and returned. Huck believed that sending it would make him a respectable boy again.

He imagined how proud people would be if they knew he had done the proper thing. The widow would approve. The town would say Huck Finn had finally behaved like a decent boy.

He remembered the nights on the raft when Jim talked about buying his wife and children out of slavery someday. Huck remembered how Jim had stayed awake to watch over him during storms. He remembered Jim calling him his only true friend.

questions.question: Why does Huck ultimately tear up the letter?

options.text: He believes the letter might not reach Miss Watson safely.
He realises that helping Jim matters more than society’s expectations.
He becomes afraid that Jim will stop trusting him.
He decides that the river journey is more exciting than returning home.

questions.question: Which statement best expresses a universal theme suggested by this passage?

options.text: True morality sometimes requires individuals to question society’s rules.
Adventures on the river always lead to personal freedom.
People should always follow the laws of their community.
Friendship is only valuable when it benefits both people equally.

questions.story:
Adapted from The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn by Mark Twain

Huck Finn sat alone with the letter in his hand, staring at the words he had written. The candle flickered beside him as the river moved quietly outside. The letter was addressed to Miss Watson, telling her where Jim was hiding.

Jim had run away from slavery, and Huck had helped him escape. Everyone Huck knew had taught him that helping a runaway slave was wrong. If Miss Watson received the letter, Jim would be captured and returned. Huck believed that sending it would make him a respectable boy again.

questions.question: Which detail from the passage provides the strongest evidence supporting the theme?

options.text: Huck imagines how proud the town would be of him.
Huck remembers that Jim stayed awake to watch over him during storms.
Huck writes the letter to Miss Watson about Jim’s location.
Huck tears the letter into pieces and decides to help Jim anyway.

questions.question:
Choose the correct form of the adjective to complete the sentence:
This explanation is ______ than the one given earlier.

options.text: clear
clearer
clearest
more clearer

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses a comparative adjective?

options.text: This route is more shorter than the highway.
This route is shortest than the highway.
This route is shorter than the highway.
This route is most short than the highway.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that correctly uses the superlative degree.

options.text: She is the most intelligent student in the class.
She is the more intelligent student in the class.
She is intelligentest student in the class.
She is more intelligent than everyone in the class most.

questions.question: Which sentence contains an error in the degree of comparison?

options.text: This mountain is higher than the nearby hill.
Mount Everest is the highest peak in the world.
This mountain is the more higher than the nearby hill.
The hill is high but not difficult to climb.

questions.question:
Select the correct adjective form to complete the sentence:
Among all the paintings in the gallery, this one appears the ______.

options.text: bright
brighter
brightest
more bright

questions.question:
Choose the correct comparative adverb to complete the sentence:
During the first debate, Maya responded calmly, but in the final round she responded ______.

options.text: calmest
more calmly
calmer
calmly

questions.question:
Select the correct superlative adverb to complete the sentence:
Among all the musicians in the orchestra, Daniel played ______ during the final movement.

options.text: more skillfully
skillful
most skillfully
skillfully

questions.question:
Choose the correct option using the irregular adverb form.
Of all the speakers at the conference, she presented her ideas ______.

options.text: more well
best
most better
wellest

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses a comparative adverb without grammatical errors?

options.text: The new student adapted more quickly than the others in the class.
The new student adapted most quickly than the others in the class.
The new student adapted more quicker than the others in the class.
The new student adapted quicklier than the others in the class.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences shows correct subject–verb agreement?

options.text: The group of tourists enjoy the view from the cliff.
The group of tourists enjoys the view from the cliff.
The group of tourists enjoying the view from the cliff.
Tourists enjoys the views from the cliff.

questions.question:
Three of the following sentences follow the rules of subject–verb agreement.
Which one is incorrect?

options.text: Bread and butter are served every morning.
The author and the illustrator work closely together.
Time and tide wait for no one.
The teacher and the principal discusses the issue.

questions.question: Which sentence contains a subject–verb agreement error?

options.text: Either the librarian or the assistants organise the records.
Either the assistants or the librarian organises the records.
Neither the musicians nor the conductor agrees with the decision.
Neither the conductor nor the musicians agrees with the decision.

questions.question:
The following sentence has an agreement error. Choose the correct replacement for the verb.
“Each of the students in the debate club have prepared a speech.”

options.text: has
were
are
have been

questions.question:
Fill in the blank with the correct verb.
“Everyone in the audience ______ eager to hear the final results.”

options.text: were
are
was
have been

questions.question: Which sentence correctly follows the rules of subject–verb agreement?

options.text: A number of students has volunteered for the project.
A number of students have volunteered for the project.
The number of students have volunteered for the project.
The number of students are volunteering for the project.

questions.question:
Choose the correct verb to complete the sentence.
Each of the proposals ______ carefully reviewed before the final decision.

options.text: were
been
was
are

questions.question: Which of the following sentences contains a subject–verb agreement error?

options.text: The committee has reached its final decision.
A pair of gloves were left on the table.
Mathematics is considered a challenging subject.
The news sounds surprising.

questions.question:
Select the correct verb to complete the sentence.
Three hours ______ enough time to complete the exam.

options.text: are
were
is
been

questions.question: Which sentence shows correct subject–verb agreement in a series of verbs?

options.text: The scientist <u>observes</u> the data, <u>record</u> the results, and <u>publish</u> the findings.
The scientist <u>observes</u> the data, <u>records</u> the results, and <u>publishes</u> the findings.
The scientist <u>observes</u> the data, <u>records</u> the results, and <u>publishes</u> the findings.
The scientist <u>observes</u> the data, <u>record</u> the results, and <u>publish</u> the findings.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses the Present Continuous Tense to describe an action happening at the moment of speaking?

options.text: She <u>studies</u> marine biology at the university.
She <u>is studying</u> marine biology in the library right now.
She <u>studied</u> marine biology yesterday evening.
She <u>has studied</u> marine biology for three years.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses the Simple Present Tense to describe a regular habit?

options.text: He <u>is jogging</u> in the park right now.
He <u>jogs</u> in the park every morning before school.
He <u>jogged</u> in the park yesterday morning.
He <u>has jogged</u> in the park several times this week.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences contains an incorrect use of the Present Continuous Tense?

options.text: They <u>are discussing</u> the proposal in the meeting right now.
She <u>is preparing</u> dinner while listening to music.
I <u>am knowing</u> the answer to this question.
We <u>are reviewing</u> the report this afternoon.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses the Simple Present Tense to describe a general fact or universal truth?

options.text: The Earth <u>revolves</u> around the Sun.
The Earth <u>is revolving</u> around the Sun today.
The Earth <u>revolved</u> around the Sun yesterday.
The Earth <u>has revolved</u> around the Sun last year.

topics.heading: Present Tense Part 2 (Perfect and Perfect Continuous)

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses the present perfect tense?

options.text: The researcher <u>has discover</u> a new method for analysing the samples.
The researcher <u>has discovered</u> a new method for analysing the samples.
The researcher <u>has discovering</u> a new method for analysing the samples.
The researcher <u>discovered</u> a new method for analysing the samples.

questions.question: Which of the following sentences is correctly written in the present perfect continuous tense?

options.text: The students <u>have study</u> for the exam since early morning.
The students <u>have been studying</u> for the exam since early morning.
The students <u>had been studying</u> for the exam since early morning.
The students <u>are been studying</u> for the exam since early morning.

questions.question:
Choose the correct verb form to complete the sentence:
The engineers ______ completed the safety inspection ahead of schedule.

options.text: has
have
is
been

questions.question:
Complete the sentence using the correct form of the verb:
Scientists have been ______ climate data from the region for several months.

options.text: collect
collected
collecting
collects

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses the simple past tense?

options.text: He <u>finished</u> the experiment before the class ended.
He <u>did finished</u> the experiment before the class ended.
He <u>was finish</u> the experiment before the class ended.
He <u>finishing</u> the experiment before the class ended.

questions.question: Which sentence properly uses the past continuous tense?

options.text: She <u>was preparing</u> the presentation when the phone rang.
She <u>were preparing</u> the presentation when the phone rang.
She <u>preparing</u> the presentation when the phone rang.
She <u>did preparing</u> the presentation when the phone rang.

questions.question:
Fill in the blank with the correct verb to complete the sentence in simple past tense.
The team ______ not understand the instructions during the meeting.

options.text: did
was
were
has

questions.question:
Fill in the blank to form a past continuous tense sentence.
They ______ discussing the proposal when the manager entered the room.

options.text: were
was
did
had

topics.heading: Past Tense Part 2 (Perfect and Perfect Continuous)

questions.question: Which of the following sentences correctly uses the Past Perfect Tense?

options.text: The scientist <u>had discovered</u> the error after the experiment ended.
The scientist <u>had discovering</u> the error after the experiment ended.
The scientist <u>had discover </u>the error after the experiment ended.
The scientist<u> had been discover</u> the error after the experiment ended.

questions.question: Which sentence correctly uses the Past Perfect Continuous Tense?

options.text: The workers <u>had repairing</u> the bridge for hours before the storm arrived.
The workers <u>had been repairing</u> the bridge for hours before the storm arrived.
The workers <u>had been repaired</u> the bridge for hours before the storm arrived.
The workers <u>had repaired</u> the bridge for hours before the storm arrived.

questions.question:
Choose the correct verb form to complete the sentence:
By the time the guests arrived, the chef ______ the entire meal.

options.text: had prepared
prepared
was preparing
prepares

questions.question:
Fill in the blank with the correct form for the Past Perfect Continuous Tense:
The researchers had been ______ the data for several months before they published their findings.

options.text: analyze
analyzed
analyzing
analyzes
